hero.title: Run your store —
hero.subtitle: KPOS brings sales, credit (debt), inventory, supply and your team together in one simple system. Built for market and shop retail.
hero.note: No credit card required · Standard — first month free

features.title: Everything you need to run sales
features.subtitle: From checkout to reports — your daily work in one place.

credit.title: Credit (debt) management
credit.desc: Sell on credit; debts, due dates and balances are tracked automatically. The single most important feature for a market seller.

pos.title: Fast checkout (POS)
pos.desc: Check out in seconds with a barcode scanner. Cash, card and credit payments supported.

inventory.title: Inventory control
inventory.desc: Stock decreases automatically on every sale. Spot low-stock items in time.

reports.title: Reports and analytics
reports.desc: Track sales, revenue and product performance in real time. See which products earn the most.

suppliers.title: Supply and receiving
suppliers.desc: Manage suppliers, record goods receipts and restock your warehouse.

team.title: Team and roles
team.desc: Create separate accounts for staff and grant each the permissions they need.

ops.title: More than a cash register
ops.subtitle: Close the shift, control the money, count the stock — even offline. The whole back office in one system.

shift.title: Cashier shifts
shift.desc: Open and close shifts. At day's end cash is reconciled against the system — Z-report and discrepancies.

finance.title: Finance ledger
finance.desc: Income, expense and transfers in one ledger. Cash, card and currency accounts — balances in real time.

stocktake.title: Stock-take
stocktake.desc: Count the real stock. Surplus and shortage are computed automatically and the balance is corrected.

offline.title: Works offline
offline.desc: Keep selling when the internet drops. On reconnect, sales sync automatically with no duplicates.

currency.title: Multi-currency
currency.desc: Work in som and dollars — across sales, cash and finance, at the day's rate.

returns.title: Supplier returns
returns.desc: Return defective goods to the supplier — stock and debt are adjusted automatically.

diff.title: Credit — the problem we solved best
diff.desc: In big POS systems credit is an afterthought. In KPOS it's central — because credit is what market and shop retail runs on.
points: Every customer's debt and payment history in one place
Turn a sale into a credit record directly
Remaining debt and total credit always in view

pricing.title: A plan that fits your business
pricing.subtitle: From a small shop to a multi-branch network — growing with you.
pricing.annualNote: −20% on annual billing · Free install and setup · Cancel anytime

proplus.name: Business+
features: Unlimited branches — +150,000 UZS each new one

free.q: Can I try it for free?
free.a: Yes. Standard's first month is free — no card required. If it's not for you, cancel anytime.

install.a: Install and initial setup are free. We help you get the system running.

credit.a: You record a sale on credit — the customer's debt is calculated automatically. Due dates, balances and payment history all live on one screen.

branches.a: Yes. Paid plans support multiple branches — up to 1 + 3 on Standard, 1 + 5 on Business, unlimited on Business+. Each additional branch is +150,000 UZS/month. All managed from one panel.

data.a: Your data is stored securely and visible only to you and the staff you grant access to.

cta.title: Bring order to your sales today
cta.subtitle: Sign up in a few minutes and make your first sale.

contact.title: Have questions?
contact.subtitle: Message or call us — we'll help you get set up for free.

inventory.costingDescription: How the cost of goods sold is valued, and what happens to the selling price of existing stock when a receipt arrives at a higher price.
inventory.keepOld: Keep old price for existing stock
inventory.title: Stock levels
inventory.whatIsInventoryDescription: Inventory is the verification of the presence of an organization's property and the state of its financial obligations on a certain date by comparing actual data with accounting data. This is the main way of actual control over the safety of property values and funds.
inventory.whyImportantDescription: According to statistics, the average rate of losses due to theft and robbery in retail is 2% of turnover. If your store makes 100 million sums per month, you lose an average of 2 million sums per month, which is 24 million per year. The less often you do inventory, the higher the chance that the rate of theft and losses will move into the risk zone and grow rapidly.
inventory.goal1Description: Check quantity and articles to see discrepancies and correct the database
inventory.goal2Description: Identify shortages and overages to reduce shrinkage to normal levels
inventory.goal3Description: Find excess goods that can be sold or returned
inventory.goal4Description: Identify slow-moving and fast-moving goods to optimize your product range
inventory.fullInventoryDescription: Complete check of all goods in the store
inventory.partialInventoryDescription: Check of specific categories or groups of goods
inventory.step1Description: Prepare all necessary documents and equipment
inventory.step3Description: Compare actual data with accounting records
inventory.benefit1Description: Conduct inventory even in areas with weak signal
inventory.benefit2Description: No need to close the store and lose income during inventory
inventory.benefit4Description: Complete full inventory in 2-3 hours instead of several days
inventory.benefit5Description: Support for multiple devices simultaneously to speed up the process
inventory.benefit6Description: Detailed reports on shortages and overages with item history
inventory.benefit7Description: Complete history of all inventories stored in the system

costingGuide.title: Cost accounting — terms
costingGuide.subtitle: A short explanation of the concepts used when receiving goods and calculating profit.
costingGuide.cogsDesc: The purchase cost of the goods you sold. Profit = revenue − COGS. It is captured at the moment of sale, so reports don't change if prices change later.
costingGuide.batchDesc: A delivery of stock with a specific purchase and selling price. Every receipt creates a batch. A sale consumes batches in order (oldest first).
costingGuide.fifoDesc: COGS is taken from the oldest batch first (first in, first out). Reflects the real purchase price of each unit. Useful when prices rise.
costingGuide.avgDesc: COGS is the averaged purchase price of all stock on hand. Smooths out price swings. This is the system default.
costingGuide.exampleIntro: Batch 1: 3 pcs at $9. Batch 2: 5 pcs at $10. You sell 5 pcs:
costingGuide.priceDesc: Each batch has its own selling price. With FIFO the old stock sells at its (old) price first, the new stock at the new price.
costingGuide.repriceTerm: When a receipt arrives at a higher price
costingGuide.repriceDesc: "Keep old price for existing stock" — old stock sells at the old price, new at the new. "Raise price for existing stock" — all stock is repriced to the new price immediately.
costingGuide.note: The costing method and price behavior are set for the whole business in the receipts section (the "Settings" button).
